description:
Discovered while implementing dsrs-vn6.1.3 (PyO3 code generation).

## Problem

Error handling in crates/rlm-derive/src/ is inconsistent between modules:

1. **lib.rs**: Properly uses syn::Error::new_spanned() to preserve source spans
2. **rlm_attr.rs**: 
   - Silently ignores parse errors (line 16: let _ = attr.parse_nested_meta(...))
   - Uses proc_macro2::Span::call_site() which loses source location

## Impact

- Users get poor error messages when #[rlm_type] attributes have syntax errors
- Error locations point to the macro call site instead of the actual error location

## Suggested Fix

1. Convert rlm_attr::expand to return syn::Result<TokenStream> 
2. Propagate errors from parse_nested_meta with proper spans
3. Use spans from the original tokens instead of Span::call_site()

## Files

- crates/rlm-derive/src/rlm_attr.rs (primary)
- crates/rlm-derive/src/lib.rs (reference for correct pattern)
title: Inconsistent error handling in rlm-derive

acceptance_criteria:
1. Predict<S> struct with PhantomData<S>
2. new() constructor
3. builder() with demo and tool methods
4. call() returns Result<S, PredictError>
5. call_with_meta() returns Result<CallResult<S>, PredictError>
6. Demos formatted correctly
7. Tools passed to LM call
8. Errors mapped to PredictError variants
title: Phase 5: Predict<S> Typed Predictor
